title: Boiling German Water Broth
description: A light, peppery water broth originating from Germany, typically consumed during monsoons. This recipe highlights a macrobiotic-friendly take on the classic German dish.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a high-mineral salt for best flavor

ingredients.name: dill weed
ingredients.notes: add a few fresh sprigs for a refreshing twist

instructions: Pour water into a medium saucepan and place it over high heat.
Add salt and black pepper to the water and stir to combine.
Bring the water to a rolling boil, using a timer to ensure the water reaches a minimum temperature of 100°C (212°F).
Reduce heat to a low simmer and allow the broth to cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add fresh dill weed sprigs to the broth during the last 2 minutes of cooking.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the broth steep for 1 minute before serving.
